text.title: C# .NET Core Backend Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree mapping observable signals to a rule from 01-core-rules.xml.

conclusion.verdict: skip-this-methodology — use `dotnet new console`; no Controller/Service/Repository imposition

branch.question: Does every async controller action carry CancellationToken threaded to IO?

conclusion.verdict: block diff until every async action accepts CancellationToken

branch.question: Does any singleton (BackgroundService) inject a scoped service via constructor?

conclusion.verdict: refactor singleton to inject IServiceScopeFactory + CreateScope per work unit

branch.question: Do repositories return IQueryable or skip AsNoTracking on reads?

conclusion.verdict: materialize lists inside repo + add AsNoTracking on reads
apply remaining rules — feature folders + ProblemDetails + ban sync-over-async
skip-this-methodology — use MediatR + Marten/EventStore directly; this umbrella's Repository pattern fights the grain

text.example: shape=http_api → cancellation_threaded=true → scoped_in_singleton=false → ef_violations=false → apply remaining rules → ProblemDetails on + feature folders + no sync-over-async.
